front: Philosopher: this thinker's magnum opus begins with a discourse on the 'forgetting of Being' in Western thought and introduces the concept of Dasein
work: Being and Time (Dasein and Fundamental Ontology)

front: Concept: the example of reaching for a hammer illustrates the distinction between two modes of encountering objects: practical engagement vs. theoretical contemplation
work: The Hammer and Ready-to-Hand vs. Present-at-Hand

front: Philosopher: used the term 'care' (Sorge) to describe an orientation toward death, and the concept of 'thrownness' to describe humans being cast into the world
work: Being and Time (Dasein and Fundamental Ontology)

front: Philosopher: discussed how a person's self is neutered by 'Das Man' ('the they') in his magnum opus
work: Being and Time (Dasein and Fundamental Ontology)

front: Work: argues that art creates strife between 'Earth' and 'World,' beginning with an Ursprung or 'primal leap'; examines Van Gogh's painting of peasant shoes
back: The Origin of the Work of Art (Heidegger)
work: The Origin of the Work of Art

front: Work: uses a Greek temple as an example of something that has lost the ability to effect the struggle between 'Earth' and 'World'
back: The Origin of the Work of Art (Heidegger)
work: The Origin of the Work of Art

front: Concept: Heidegger's translation of this Greek term as 'unconcealedness' (rather than propositional correctness) identifies it as the essence of art, since art discloses a world
work: The Origin of the Work of Art

front: Work: argues the essence of technology is 'enframing' (Gestell), which makes the world a 'standing reserve'
work: The Question Concerning Technology

front: Work: uses the example of a silversmith creating a sacrificial vessel to argue that Aristotle's four causes do not account for poesis
work: The Question Concerning Technology

front: Philosopher: the quote 'the nothing itself nothings' from this thinker's lecture is frequently criticized as an example of nonsense
work: What is Metaphysics? and 'The Nothing Nothings'

front: Philosopher: the 2014 publication of this thinker's Black Notebooks revealed further antisemitism, including that Jews were 'worldless' and 'uprooted from Being-in-the-World'
work: Nazism, Antisemitism, and the Black Notebooks

front: Philosopher: gave the speech 'The Self-Assertion of the German University' as rector of Freiburg, claiming science must shape the university and that academic freedom was 'not genuine'
work: Nazism, Antisemitism, and the Black Notebooks

front: Philosopher: Victor Farias wrote a controversial book attacking this thinker's ideas; Emmanuel Faye published a further jeremiad
work: Nazism, Antisemitism, and the Black Notebooks

front: Philosopher: Keiji Nishitani studied Nietzsche with this thinker for two years and compared Zen practice to this thinker's definition of aletheia as 'unveiling'
work: Connections to Eastern Philosophy and the Kyoto School

front: Philosopher: claimed D.T. Suzuki's work was 'what I have been trying to say in all my writings'
work: Connections to Eastern Philosophy and the Kyoto School

front: Philosopher: debated Ernst Cassirer at Davos in 1929; argued freedom meant 'becoming free for the finitude of existence'
work: Davos Debate with Cassirer

front: Philosopher: Adorno criticized this thinker's language as mystifying in Jargon of Authenticity
work: Later Works and Influence

front: Philosopher: Graham Harman developed object-oriented philosophy / 'guerrilla metaphysics' based on this thinker's work
work: Later Works and Influence

front: Philosopher: Derrida borrowed this thinker's technique of sous rature (erasure), striking out a word but leaving it visible
work: Later Works and Influence

front: Philosopher: Richard Rorty praised this thinker (along with Wittgenstein and Dewey) as 'edifying' in Philosophy and the Mirror of Nature
work: Later Works and Influence

front: Philosopher: originated the dictum 'language speaks' and said 'language is the house of being'
work: Later Works and Influence

front: Philosopher: Hubert Dreyfus's commentaries on this thinker led to a portmanteau of their names; Dreyfus applied this thinker's ideas to criticize artificial intelligence
work: Later Works and Influence

front: Philosopher: later works discuss the 'fourfold' — the oneness of earth, sky, divinities, and mortals
work: Later Works and Influence

front: Philosopher: Tomonobu Imamichi claimed this thinker took the idea of Dasein from Japanese teacher Okakura Kakuzo, author of The Book of Tea
work: Connections to Eastern Philosophy and the Kyoto School
